article_title: An unusual case of Cardiobacterium valvarum causing aortic endograft infection and osteomyelitis
journal: Annals of Clinical Microbiology and Antimicrobials
label: Fig. 1
caption: Magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) of the lumbar spine demonstrating discitis and vertebral osteomyelitis. T1 post-contrast sagittal MRI demonstrating enhancement at the L3–4 intervertebral disc space with erosions in the adjacent endplates (arrow)

article_title: An unusual case of Cardiobacterium valvarum causing aortic endograft infection and osteomyelitis
journal: Annals of Clinical Microbiology and Antimicrobials
label: Fig. 2
caption: Gram staining. Microscopic morphology in gram staining of blood culture after 96 h of aerobic incubation at 37 °C demonstrating bipolar-staining gram-negative bacilli. 16S ribosomal RNA (rRNA) sequencing subsequently identified the organism as Cardiobacterium valvarum

article_title: An unusual case of Cardiobacterium valvarum causing aortic endograft infection and osteomyelitis
journal: Annals of Clinical Microbiology and Antimicrobials
label: Fig. 3
caption: Positron emission tomography–computed tomography (PET/CT) of the chest. Hypermetabolic soft tissue (arrows) along the right lateral and anterior aspect of the ascending aortic endograft with a maximum standardized uptake value of 9.4, suspicious for aortic endograft infection

article_title: Management of Refractory Post-operative Osteomyelitis and Discitis: A Case Report
label: Figure 1
caption: Pre-operative MRI Scan of Lumbar Spine, with Arrows Denoting L3/4 Disc HerniationPre-operative MRI demonstrating multilevel spondylosis and a focal disc herniation at L3-4.
